review_notes: The functions map well to Kubernetes audit logging capabilities, which are relevant for non-repudiation by ensuring actions are logged with user identities. However, the function name 'k8s_audit_log_identity_binding_validated' was slightly ambiguous and has been refined to better reflect its purpose of validating user identities in audit logs.
naming_improvements: Use 'user_identity' instead of 'identity_binding' for clarity in function names
title: Non-Repudiation - Non - repudiation | validate binding of information reviewer identity (a) valida
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 AU-10(4) control focuses on non-repudiation by validating the identity of information reviewers. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to audit logging practices that ensure actions are logged with sufficient detail to validate user identities. Kubernetes audit logs can be configured to capture metadata, including user identities, which supports non-repudiation. Additionally, ensuring that audit logs are retained for a specific period helps in validating historical actions.
